framework.name: Verifiable Credential-Based Agent Authentication
framework.description: AP2 uses verifiable credentials (VCs) to cryptographically sign mandates, creating a chain of evidence for authorization, authenticity, and accountability in agent transactions. This establishes non-repudiable audit trails that prove user intent and agent authority.
framework.raw_quote: Mandates—tamper-proof, cryptographically-signed digital contracts that serve as verifiable proof of a user's instructions. These mandates are signed by verifiable credentials (VCs) and act as the foundational evidence for every transaction... This complete sequence—from intent, to cart, to payment—creates a non-repudiable audit trail that answers the critical questions of authorization and authenticity.

source.title: Announcing Agent Payments Protocol (AP2) | Google Cloud Blog
